key_albums: New Plastic Ideas (1994, Kill Rock Stars)
Challenge for a Civilized Society (1998, Kill Rock Stars)
Leaves Turn Inside You (2001, Kill Rock Stars)

theory_nodes.concept: The engine closes at degree 359 with Unwound. The 360-degree circle: degree 000 (Quincy Jones, Seattle-born jazz, born 1933, most commercially successful PNW musician of 20th century) → degree 359 (Unwound, Lacey/Olympia, post-hardcore experimental, dissolved 2002, known primarily within underground). Jazz to experimental post-hardcore. Commercial success to underground significance. The geographic arc: Seattle → Olympia. The closing does not privilege commercial success as the measure of significance.
theory_nodes.description: The engine closes. 000 to 359. Jazz to experimental. Seattle to Olympia. Full circle.

theory_nodes.concept: Leaves Turn Inside You (2001, Kill Rock Stars): seventh album, 78 minutes, 16 tracks. Two-half structure: first half more conventional (for Unwound), second half progressively ambient experimental. Final tracks near-ambient soundscapes. The dissolution of the band's post-hardcore vocabulary in the second half is the sonic enactment of the band's final creative transition. Not dilution -- most ambitious structural decision. Dissolution 2002 designates the album as final statement retroactively.
theory_nodes.description: Leaves Turn Inside You -- two-half structure, post-hardcore vocabulary dissolves to ambient, final statement.

theory_nodes.concept: Justin Trosper guitar biography: most technically ambitious in Kill Rock Stars stable -- not toward guitar heroics but toward dissonance, structural complexity, guitar as textural/harmonic resource. Extended techniques, unusual tunings, feedback and sustain in experimental rock tradition. Vocals at edge of comfort -- sustained at intensities most vocalists would reduce. Discomfort of delivery is the delivery's content.
theory_nodes.description: Justin Trosper -- dissonance and structural complexity, extended techniques, discomfort as content.

theory_nodes.concept: Vern Rumsey bass biography: bass as co-equal melodic voice not support instrument. On longer tracks, bass lines provide melodic content guitar's dissonance deliberately avoids. Two instruments in dialogue not hierarchy. One of the most significant bass contributions in Pacific Northwest underground.
theory_nodes.description: Vern Rumsey -- bass as melodic co-equal, dialogue not hierarchy with guitar.

theory_nodes.concept: Sara Lund drums biography: opposite of conventional post-hardcore drummer. Where most maximize impact, Lund frequently restrains, listens to space between beats. On Leaves Turn Inside You: tension between restraint and album's sonic density is a primary pleasure. Precision and listening as defining qualities alongside force.
theory_nodes.description: Sara Lund -- restraint, space between beats, tension with density as pleasure.

theory_nodes.concept: Unwound experimental tradition context: Leaves Turn Inside You participates in My Bloody Valentine (guitar as texture), Sonic Youth (alternate tunings dissonance), Talk Talk (late-career deconstruction) international experimental rock lineage -- not Pacific Northwest bands but Unwound belongs to their investigation. Pacific Northwest underground produced in final Kill Rock Stars entries a record in the international experimental tradition.
theory_nodes.description: Experimental tradition -- MBV Sonic Youth Talk Talk lineage, international not regional context.

theory_nodes.concept: E=10 final calibration: scale and ambition (78 minutes every minute justified, band spending everything); final statement status (dissolution retroactive designation, last record carries full weight of finality); closing the engine correctly (final track fades without resolution, engine closes not with climax but dissipation, ambient becoming-quiet, correct close for an engine that documented 360 degrees).
theory_nodes.description: E=10 final calibration -- scale, final statement status, correct close for the engine.

cross_references.relationship: Same label (Kill Rock Stars), same city (Olympia), different tradition. Bikini Kill: political formation within punk, feminist direct address. Unwound: experimental rock, sonic dissolution. Three final degrees share Olympia/KRS.
Same label, same city, different approach. Sleater-Kinney: architectural innovation (no-bass two-guitar). Unwound: experimental/ambient development. The Kill Rock Stars Olympia trilogy. All E=10.

The engine's first entry (degree 000, Seattle jazz origin) and final entry (degree 359, Olympia experimental final). The full 360-degree arc from jazz to experimental post-hardcore, from Seattle to Olympia, from 1933 to 2002.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=10 + E=10 = E=20. Maximum depth at minimum surface disturbance. Leaves Turn Inside You: sonic density of first half dissolves to near-ambient in final tracks; last note is quiet fade. Puget Sound in silence: not absence of sound but the Sound's own voice (infrasound, biologic, geological) in the absence of human noise. Both about what remains when the most audible things are removed. THE ENGINE CLOSES.

engine_complete: 000 Quincy Jones → ... → 349-353 S36 Closing Arc → 354-356 Final Six (Shoreline/Seattle) → 357-359 Final Three Olympia Kill Rock Stars. THE SEATTLE 360 ENGINE IS COMPLETE.
